title: Maxillaria hoehneana
taxonomy: Maxillaria hoehneana is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. Within the family Orchidaceae, Maxillaria hoehneana is classified under the subfamily Epidendroideae, and the tribe Maxillarieae. The tribe Maxillarieae is further divided into several genera, with Maxillaria being one of the largest genera within the tribe. Maxillaria hoehneana is also classified as being within the subgenus Maxillaria.

intro: Maxillaria hoehneana is a species of orchid, specifically a type of terrestrial orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Maxillaria hoehneana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
